notes.hypothesis: After several 0.5 s captures, a short 5 s cadence experiment can verify the experiment UI can still capture at the normal slower interval and provide an operational sanity check before returning to ordinary logging.
notes.method: A 30 s capture was run at 5 s cadence with all four probes in dry/open-bench condition. This was an operational cadence check, not a calibration experiment.
notes.findings: The capture produced 5 sweeps and 20 rows with no dropped rows and no CRC failures. Readings stayed dry and stable: medians s1 3175, s2 3124, s3 3123, s4 3134 raw ADC.
notes.conclusion: The 5 s experiment capture path worked. This file is useful as an operational note that the bench returned from sub-second testing to slower cadence, but it should not be weighted heavily as a calibration anchor due to the very short duration.
